seoTitle: How to Integrate Asana With Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Connect Asana to Slack to stream project updates into channels and turn chat messages into actionable tasks without switching apps.
intro: Keeping project tracking synchronized with team chat helps avoid missed deadlines and constant tab-switching. This guide covers installing the official Asana app for Slack, linking a project to a channel, and converting messages into tasks.

steps.heading: Open the App Directory
steps.body: From the left sidebar, launch the App Marketplace in your Slack workspace.

steps.heading: Search for Asana
steps.body: Type Asana into the search field and select the verified app listing.

steps.heading: Install and authorize
steps.body: Click 'Add to Slack', review the requested permissions, and click Allow to authorize the connection.

steps.heading: Link a project to a channel
steps.body: Sign into your Asana account when redirected, return to Slack, and use the /asana link command to map a project board to a channel.

steps.heading: Turn a message into a task
steps.body: Hover over any Slack message, click the three-dot menu, and select 'Create a Task' to instantly add it to Asana.

faq.question: Will task status updates show up automatically in Slack?
faq.answer: Yes, once a project is linked to a channel, status changes and new task alerts stream in automatically.

faq.question: Can I create tasks assigned to specific teammates from Slack?
faq.answer: Yes, the task creation shortcut generally lets you set assignees and due dates before saving.

faq.question: Do I need an Asana account to use this integration?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an active Asana account with access to the project you want to connect.

faq.question: Can I unlink a project from a channel later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can remove or update the link using the same /asana command inside the channel.
